value.body:
## Context

When an agent is assigned to work on a ticket, it currently behaves like a normal chat response to the user. Instead, assigned ticket work should follow a clear implementation workflow that prepares the repository, updates ticket state, completes the work, and leaves a structured handoff.

Target repository: cycle://repository/repo_16371

## Outcome

Agents assigned to implement tickets follow a deterministic workflow:

1. Create a dedicated worktree with full read-write permissions.
2. Assign the ticket to the current user and move the ticket to `In Progress` before starting implementation.
3. Complete the work described in the ticket.
4. If an issue is discovered outside the ticket scope, create a separate ticket for that issue using a sub-agent while the main agent continues the original task.
5. After completion:
   - Push the branch to the remote when possible.
   - Move the ticket to `In Review`.
   - Create an `In Review` stage/status if it does not already exist.
   - Reply on the ticket with detailed handoff notes, links, and testing notes.

Creating a PR is explicitly outside the scope of this change.

## Acceptance criteria

- Assigned ticket implementation uses a dedicated worktree.
- The worktree is created with full read-write permissions.
- The ticket is assigned to the current user before implementation begins.
- The ticket status is changed to `In Progress` before implementation begins.
- The agent performs the implementation work described by the ticket.
- Out-of-scope follow-up issues can be captured as separate tickets without blocking the main implementation flow.
- On successful completion, the branch is pushed to the configured remote when possible.
- The ticket is moved to `In Review`.
- If no `In Review` status/stage exists, it is created or otherwise made available.
- The agent posts a ticket reply containing:
  - Summary of completed work
  - Branch/remote links where available
  - Testing performed
  - Known limitations or follow-up tickets
- PR creation is not added as part of this workflow.

## Risks

- Ticket status names may differ across repositories or boards.
- Worktree creation and permissions may need to account for sandboxed agent environments.
- Remote push may fail when credentials or network access are unavailable; this should be reported in handoff notes rather than treated as silent success.
- Creating follow-up tickets from sub-agents needs guardrails to avoid duplicate or low-quality tickets.

frontmatter.title: Implement structured agent workflow for ticket assignment execution

value.title: Implement structured agent workflow for ticket assignment execution
